title: Fix SQL Foreign Key Constraint Violation on Source Object Creation
description:
An integrity constraint violation (`SQLSTATE[23000]`) occurred when creating a source object in the `dtp_source_object` table. The foreign key constraint on `salesforce_object_id` referencing `dtp_scorecards(id)` failed because the `salesforce_object_id` was not set properly before the insert.

## Root Cause

The `salesforce_object_id` field was expected to be set when navigating to an object detail page, but under certain race conditions (e.g., object deleted in another tab), the value could be missing.

## Fix

1. Added a pre-request check: if `salesforce_object_id` is not set, display a user message "Please select an object first" instead of attempting the insert
2. Fixed a related issue where soft-deleted objects still showed their fields on the detail page — now shows "No data available" message
acceptance_criteria: Source object creation validates salesforce_object_id is set before insert
User sees 'Please select an object first' when salesforce_object_id is missing
Soft-deleted objects show 'No data available' on their detail page
Foreign key constraint violation no longer appears in error logs
ux_changes: Added user-facing messages: 'Please select an object first' when salesforce_object_id is missing, and 'No data available' when viewing a soft-deleted object's fields.
middleware_changes: Added pre-insert validation to check salesforce_object_id existence before creating source objects. Added soft-delete awareness to object detail page queries.
implementation_notes: The race condition occurred when a user opened an object detail page, then deleted the object from the dashboard in another tab, then attempted to create a source object from the stale detail page. The fix adds defensive checks at both the controller and view levels.
comment_summary: Developer could not reproduce the exact conditions but added defensive validation: a pre-request check for salesforce_object_id and soft-delete awareness on the object detail page.
